name: El Celemín by the Embalse de Benamarías – running circular from Benamarías
name_original: El Celemín por el Embalse de Benamarías – running circular desde Benamarías
region_intro: Jogging around Magaz De Cepeda offers a mix of natural and urban running experiences. The region features routes that explore the surroundings of the Embalse de Benamarías, providing paths near water bodies. Additionally, many running routes traverse the historic town of Astorga, characterized by its urban landscape and cultural landmarks. The terrain generally includes gentle inclines and mostly flat sections, suitable for various running paces. Best jogging routes around Magaz De Cepeda The most popular jogging route is El Celemín by the Embalse de Benamarías – running circular from Benamarías, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a circular path around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Street Art @ Astorga – Astorga loop from Astorga, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This route explores the urban environment of Astorga, highlighting street art and city features. Local runners also love the Gaudí Palace – Astorga loop from Astorga, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through urban streets and past historic landmarks, often completed in about 57 minutes. Jogging around Magaz De Cepeda is defined by reservoir paths, urban streets, and historic landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate urban loops to longer routes by the water. The routes in Magaz De Cepeda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Magaz De Cepeda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Magaz De Cepeda?
A: There are over 25 running routes in the Magaz De Cepeda area, offering a diverse range of experiences from urban exploration to natural reservoir paths. These include 1 easy route, 19 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Yes, the region offers at least one easy running route. Generally, the terrain around Magaz De Cepeda features gentle inclines and mostly flat sections, making many routes suitable for various running paces, including beginners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes near Magaz De Cepeda?
A: The running routes around Magaz De Cepeda offer a mix of natural and urban terrain. You'll find paths exploring the surroundings of the Embalse de Benamarías, as well as routes traversing the historic town of Astorga with its urban landscape and cultural landmarks. The terrain generally includes gentle inclines and mostly flat sections.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055946977/el-celemin-by-the-embalse-de-benamarias-running-circular-from-benamarias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Celemín by the Embalse de Benamarías – running circular from Benamarías&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route around the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while running near Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Many routes pass by significant cultural landmarks, especially those in Astorga. You can see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5898729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary of Astorga&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Episcopal Palace of Astorga (Gaudí Palace)&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Street Art @ Astorga – Astorga loop from Astorga&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights urban art and city features.

Q: Are the running trails around Magaz De Cepeda suitable for families?
A: Given the mix of gentle inclines and mostly flat sections, many of the moderate routes, especially those exploring urban areas like Astorga or the less strenuous paths around the Embalse de Benamarías, can be suitable for families. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: The running routes in Magaz De Cepeda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from scenic reservoir paths to the cultural experience of running through historic Astorga.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: While specific viewpoints are not detailed for every route, the paths around the Embalse de Benamarías offer picturesque views of the water and surrounding natural landscape. Routes through Astorga also provide scenic urban vistas of historic architecture and street art.

Q: What is the typical duration for running routes in Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and your pace. For example, a moderate 7.9-mile (12.7 km) route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055946977/el-celemin-by-the-embalse-de-benamarias-running-circular-from-benamarias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Celemín by the Embalse de Benamarías&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 1 hour 26 minutes, while a 5.6-mile (9.0 km) urban trail such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaudí Palace – Astorga loop from Astorga&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in around 57 minutes.

Q: Can I find dog-friendly running trails near Magaz De Cepeda?
A: Many natural trails, especially those outside urban centers like the paths around the Embalse de Benamarías, are generally suitable for running with dogs. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations and ensure your dog is on a leash where required, particularly in more populated or protected areas.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' running routes in the Magaz De Cepeda region?
A: While the most popular routes are well-documented, the region has over 25 routes in total, suggesting many less-trafficked options. Exploring some of the moderate or difficult routes that are not among the top-rated might lead you to discover quieter paths and unique local experiences beyond the main attractions.
tips: The route is approximately 12 kilometres long and of low difficulty, making it accessible to most hikers. It is a perfect place to enjoy nature and disconnect from the daily hustle and bustle.

The Benamarías reservoir is a lovely place to enjoy a day outdoors. It is located in the town of Benamarías, in the region of La Cepeda, Castilla y León. This reservoir is ideal for activities such as hiking, fishing and, of course, enjoying a nice picnic in its picnic area.
The picnic area has tables and benches, making it a perfect place to relax and enjoy nature. In addition, the surroundings offer beautiful views and a peaceful atmosphere, ideal for disconnecting from the daily hustle and bustle.
